notes: Core business applications: Finance/Accounting; Manufacturing/Logistics; HR Management; Sales/Distribution

Best-selling SAP product; moving from big-bang to componentized modules; Business Engineer (BE) process modeler included
Mainframe ERP; predecessor to R/3; developed by four ex-IBM employees 25 years before report
Rapid implementation methodology addressing protracted implementation complaints; supports middle market
Componentized ERP suite; includes BaanERP and Dynamic Enterprise Modeler (DEM) for process modeling
Most mature process modeling entry per Aberdeen; Baan's Target Enterprise implementation methodology included
Financials/Manufacturing/HRM; Application Wizard for process modeling; competitive with SAP; vertical market solutions via partners

Financials/Materials Management/Distribution/Supply Chain/Manufacturing/HR; DirectPath process modeling product; version 7.5 for Order Management and Manufacturing
PeopleSoft's process modeling and rapid implementation technology; competing with Baan DEM and SAP Business Engineer
Platform-independent; introduced June 1996; CASE technology foundation; Windows NT and AS/400 support

i2's APO product suite; supply chain planning/scheduling across enterprise; synchronous and asynchronous ERP/legacy integration
Pull-driven customer-demand order fulfillment; targets $250M+ companies; Consumer Packaged Goods/wholesale distribution focus
First business application supplier to capitalize on IBM's native Lotus Domino implementation on AS/400

Most functionally rich AS/400-based process manufacturing ERP solution per Aberdeen; process manufacturing focus
Flagship QAD product; global supply chain/manufacturing/distribution/financials/service; 82 countries; 30% Fortune 100 customers
Extended supply chain; 100% Java and CORBA compliant; componentized; manages complex demand/fulfillment across multi-vendor supply chain
Web-deployable; multi-level cross-organizational workflow; non-manufacturing focus; Minneapolis MN
WINTEL (Windows NT on Intel) strategy; attractive price point for mid-market; key ERP platform
Described as now a 'metaphor'; enabling e-commerce and B2B transactions; fundamental to IRP model
One of three IRP convergent technologies; component-based architectures; IBM/Microsoft/Sun infrastructure providers
Distinct emerging market per Aberdeen; integrates at business semantics/process level vs. traditional middleware; 8 named vendors
Third IRP convergent technology; multi-topology network; Internet/Intranet architecture; scaleable server hardware with RDBMS and TP-monitor middleware
Just-in-time supply chain execution adopted in early 1990s; driving need for cross-enterprise ERP
Cited as familiar integration model (spreadsheet to financial system); precursor vision for future IRP integration
